texto: You’re like that single wild flower that grows from the crack in the pavement: miraculous growth with no water source or fertile soil. A person walking by would step around that flower to avoid crushing it. It’s not like the field of wild flowers you tromp through carelessly, crushing them under your feet, knowing that the next day will bring a hundred more.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a J.B. Salsbury: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de esforço, persistência e resultados. O trecho “You’re like that single wild flower that grows from the crack in the pavement: miraculous growth …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, romance, flower pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.
